# Philosophy

## Atom index (14 atoms, auto-generated from atoms.jsonl)

_Every atom under this topic with its source proof link. Regenerate with `python -m engram topics`._

### claim (5)
- **Most programming paradigms are really just different takes on how you manage state — it's all about state.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[CORE]`
- **Computers fundamentally have state: the Von Neumann architecture is one giant state in memory with little instructions that mutate it.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`
- **Input is non-deterministic and any output forces some side effect, so pure statelessness can't fully exist.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`
- **What has happened is that the best parts of functional programming have been extracted and injected into our most popular languages.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[CORE]`
- **Massively parallel systems don't rely on one big state of memory; the more cores you add, the more tracking shared state becomes a bottleneck.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`

### opinion (6)
- **Most programming jobs are just taking data from source A, doing transformation/filtering/sorting, and putting it in place B — a lot of the time we're just sophisticated plumbers.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[CORE]`
- **Pure functional programming — where you cannot mutate any state or have any side effects — is kind of awful, and in a sense fake.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[CORE]`
- **Pure functional programming is still mostly academic.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`
- **Balance is key: adopting principles like reducing state and forming data pipelines where it makes sense brings huge benefit without the crazy restrictiveness of pure FP.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[CORE]`
- **You write code to solve a real-world problem, and getting to that solution faster is typically better than solving it slower with faster code.** — [tKbV6BpH-C8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tKbV6BpH-C8) `[CORE]`
- **Spending three hours investigating a micro-optimization is time you could have spent building the actual product.** — [tKbV6BpH-C8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tKbV6BpH-C8) `[STATED]`

### prediction (2)
- **Expect these functional patterns to become more common.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`
- **Pure functional programming may have a new renaissance as parallelism grows.** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`

### style (1)
- **Recurring affectionate address to the audience: 'dear functional bros, I see you, I hear you, and I love you.'** — [nuML9SmdbJ4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nuML9SmdbJ4) `[STATED]`
